title: Nursery Rhyme. CXXXVII. Songs.
content:
Up at Piccadilly oh!
            The coachman takes his stand,
        And when he meets a pretty girl,
            He takes her by the hand;
                Whip away for ever oh!
                Drive away so clever oh!
                All the way to Bristol oh!
        He drives her four-in-hand.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CXXXVII. Songs."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
